issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses the space between words. This can make text more difficult to read, especially for users with dyslexia or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The a selector removes underlines from all links without providing an alternative visual cue (like a border-bottom or background change). This makes it difficult for users, especially those with color vision deficiencies, to distinguish links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 100: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Font size too small on html element
issuesFound.explanation: The html selector sets font-size: 12px, which is below the recommended 16px for body text. This can make text difficult to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 94: html { scroll-behavior: smooth; text-size-adjust: 100%; -webkit-text-size-adjust: 100%; font-size: 16px; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); tab-size: 4; -moz-tab-size: 4; height: 100%; }

fpTitles: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for .hero-image img
Font size too small on html element

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open(), close(), and toggle() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users won't know if the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
In the open() function add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
       In the close() function add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions show/hide dropdown panels but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger buttons. Screen reader users won't know the expanded state of dropdown menus.
issuesFound.fix:
In _openInstance function add: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
       In _closeInstance function add: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle switch (month/year) changes visual state but does not set aria-pressed on the toggle element. Screen reader users won't know which state is selected.
issuesFound.fix: In _syncToggleState function, add: setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, isYear);

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs do not use aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons (only one active at a time) but use CSS classes instead of aria-pressed. Screen reader users won't know which filter is selected.
issuesFound.fix: In _syncTabAriaStates function, replace toggleClass with: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);

issuesFound.title: Product filter count update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count badge updates but there's no live region announcement. Screen reader users won't know how many products are shown.
issuesFound.fix: In applyFilters function, after _updateCountBadge, add: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);

issuesFound.title: Pricing price updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When billing period changes, prices update visually but there's no live region announcement. Screen reader users won't know prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: In setPeriod function, after updating prices, add: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items expand/collapse, there's no live region announcement. Screen reader users won't know when content appears/disappears.
issuesFound.fix:
In open function add: liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);
       In close function add: liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validator marks fields with CSS classes but doesn't set aria-invalid attribute. Screen readers won't announce invalid fields correctly.
issuesFound.fix:
In _renderResult function, in the invalid branch add: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);
       In the valid branch add: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button doesn't have aria-expanded set initially. Screen reader users won't know the initial state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: In init function, after setting _trigger, add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menus do not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons don't have aria-expanded set initially. Screen reader users won't know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In register function, after setting aria-haspopup, add: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

fpTitles: Dropdown menus do not initialize aria-expanded on page load

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `toggle` function in the mobile navigation module changes the visible state of the drawer but does not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen-reader users will not be informed of the menu's expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1950: `function toggle() { _open ? close() : open(); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, _open); }`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onToggleClick` function in the pricing module changes the billing period state but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the toggle switch element. Screen-reader users cannot perceive the toggle's pressed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2849: `function _onToggleClick() { const next = _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'month' : 'year'; setPeriod(next); setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, next === 'year'); }`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateCountBadge` function updates the product count badge text content but does not announce this change via an ARIA live region. Screen-reader users will not hear the updated count when filtering products.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2471: `function _updateCountBadge(count) { if (!_countBadge) return; _countBadge.textContent = String(count); setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-label', `${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''}`); liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`); }`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the inline validator adds error classes but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid fields. Screen-reader users will not be informed of the field's invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3319: `function _renderResult(field, result, errorEl) { if (result.valid) { ... removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID); } else { ... setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true); } }`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have an initial `aria-expanded` attribute set in the DOM or during module initialisation. Screen readers may not correctly report the button's initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1942: `function init() { ... setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); }`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open/close/toggle functions change the visible state of the drawer but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot know if the menu is expanded or collapsed, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open function, add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); In mobileNav.close function, add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Product view mode toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The grid/list view toggle buttons change visual state but do not set aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot tell which view is active, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In productFilter._onViewToggle function, add: setAttr(b, ARIA.PRESSED, b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === mode);

issuesFound.title: Dynamic product filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, product count and grid update visibly but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed of the content change, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: In productFilter.applyFilters function, after updating count, add: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors do not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are invalid, error messages are displayed but aria-invalid is not set on the input. Screen reader users are not alerted to the invalid state, violating WCAG 4.1.2 and 3.3.1.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator._renderResult function, when result.valid is false, add: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true); when valid, add: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button lacks an initial aria-expanded attribute when the page loads with the menu closed. Screen reader users start with unknown menu state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init function, add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers do not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are created without an initial aria-expanded attribute when panels are hidden. Screen reader users cannot know the initial collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.register function, add: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When billing period changes, price updates are visible but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed of the dynamic change, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: In pricing.setPeriod function, add: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing${period === 'year' ? ' — save 20%' : ''}`);

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons lose their outline on focus‑visible and no other visual cue (e.g., box‑shadow) is defined, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which button is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the outline on focus‑visible and provides no alternative visual focus style, breaking keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 380: `.nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons remove the outline on focus‑visible without any replacement styling, leaving keyboard users without a visible focus marker.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560: `.filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons lose their outline on focus‑visible and do not provide an alternative visual cue, hindering keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: `.faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: -3px; /* add visible focus style */ box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle buttons remove the outline on focus‑visible without any replacement, making it unclear which toggle is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 800: `.pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove the outline on focus‑visible and do not supply an alternative focus style, breaking accessibility for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 860: `.social-links a:focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets a minimum height of 20 px, which is below the WCAG‑recommended 44 px target size, making it hard to tap on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 250: `.btn-sm { min-height: 32px; /* increase to meet 44 px minimum (including padding) */ }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon‑only buttons have a minimum width and height of 20 px, below the 44 px minimum target size, reducing tapability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 260: `.btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector applies “word-spacing: -0.2em”, which can cause words to overlap and hinder readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 140: `body { word-spacing: normal; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state of hero images animates with a 3‑D transform, but no reduced‑motion media query disables this motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 420: `.hero-image img:hover { transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(0deg) rotateX(0deg); }`  
  Additional fix (in reduced‑motion block):  
  `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text on a
issuesFound.explanation: Global “a { text-decoration: none; }” removes underlines from all links without providing another non‑colour visual cue, making links hard to identify for users with low colour perception.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 120: `a { text-decoration: underline; }`  
  (or add a non‑colour cue, e.g., `a { border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; }`)

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing any visible replacement (e.g., box‑shadow or border). Keyboard users receive no visual cue when focus moves to interactive elements.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule removes the outline and does not supply an alternative visual focus style, leaving button focus invisible for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button:focus-visible selector removes the outline without any replacement, so focus on filter buttons is not perceivable.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible rule sets outline: none and outline-offset: -3px without any visible alternative, making focus on FAQ toggles invisible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector removes the outline and does not provide any other visible focus styling, so the mobile navigation toggle lacks a focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a:focus-visible rule removes the outline without supplying a replacement, leaving link focus invisible for assistive technology users.

issuesFound.title: Visually‑hidden utility (.sr-only) does not hide from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and does not set the required clip or size properties to truly hide content from visual layout while keeping it accessible. This defeats its purpose for screen‑reader‑only text.

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing on body text
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets word-spacing: -0.2em; which can reduce readability, especially for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 210:   word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Global link underline removed without alternative styling
issuesFound.explanation: The rule a { text-decoration: none; } removes the default underline from all links, and no other visual cue (color change, border, background) is provided for the majority of links, making them indistinguishable from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: Transform animation on .btn:active lacks reduced‑motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:active selector applies transform: translateY(1px); which moves the element. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override is provided, violating users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .nav-toggle (28 px)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button has min-height: 28px; and min-width: 28px; which is below the WCAG‑recommended 44 × 44 px touch target size, making it hard for finger navigation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .filter-tabs button (28 px)
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons have min-height: 28px; which is below the 44 px minimum, reducing tap accuracy.

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on generic .btn (32 px)
issuesFound.explanation: The base .btn class sets min-height: 32px; which is below the recommended 44 px touch target size, affecting all button variants that inherit this rule.

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .social-links a (28 px)
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links have min-height: 28px; which is below the 44 px minimum, making them difficult to tap.

fpTitles: Touch target too small on generic .btn (32 px)

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button lacks explicit relationship to drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle button does not have an `aria-controls` attribute referencing the drawer element, so screen readers cannot locate the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 1027 (see Issue 1) the `aria-controls` attribute pointing to the drawer’s id (generated with `srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, "nav-drawer")`).

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input does not expose its expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The search `<input>` is turned into a combobox (`role="combobox"`), but the script never sets `aria‑expanded` to reflect whether the suggestion listbox is visible. This breaks the combobox pattern (WCAG 2.1 (ARIA1)).
issuesFound.fix: In `siteSearch.init()` after `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');` add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` and toggle it to `'true'` in `_open()` and back to `'false'` in `_close()`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open routine does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is opened, the script updates classes but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button, leaving the state ambiguous for AT.
issuesFound.fix: In `faqAccordion.open(item)` after `item.open = true;` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change is not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates price numbers but does not send a live‑region announcement, so users of assistive technology are unaware of the new pricing.

issuesFound.title: Toggle widgets do not initialise aria‑expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Several interactive components (mobile nav, site‑search, FAQ accordion, pricing toggle) are hidden by default but the script never sets `aria-expanded="false"` on their triggers during initialisation, violating the ARIA design pattern requirement that the state be explicit on load.
issuesFound.fix:
In each component’s `init()` after locating the trigger element, add `setAttr(triggerEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`. For the FAQ accordion, ensure each trigger gets this attribute if the item is initially closed. For the pricing toggle, set `aria-pressed="false"` as in Issue 5.  

---  

These eight issues satisfy the mandatory JavaScript ARIA sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑E) and address the most critical accessibility gaps identified in the code.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function shows the off‑canvas drawer but never sets aria‑expanded on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Without this ARIA state, screen‑reader users cannot know whether the navigation is open or closed, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (ARIA state synchronization).
issuesFound.fix: In open() add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in close() add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`. Also ensure the trigger has `aria-controls` pointing to the drawer ID.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open action does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is opened, the code animates the panel and updates ARIA controls and describedby, but it never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button (only false is set on close). This leaves the trigger’s state ambiguous for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of open().

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle buttons do not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period buttons (`[data-period]`) receive visual active class changes, but their aria‑pressed state is never synchronized. Users of assistive technology cannot tell which period is active.
issuesFound.fix: In _syncToggleState() loop through _periodBtns and call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btnPeriod===period));` or use `toggleAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', true/false)` accordingly.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The badge showing the number of matching products is updated visually, but no ARIA live region is used to announce the new count. Users relying on screen readers receive no feedback that the result set changed.
issuesFound.fix: Add a hidden <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> element (e.g., `#filter-count-status`) and update its textContent inside _updateCountBadge() (e.g., `statusEl.textContent = `${count} product${count!==1?'s':''} found`;`).

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The validator adds error messages and CSS classes but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the offending field, nor clears it on success. This prevents assistive technologies from recognizing invalid inputs, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In validate() after a failed validateField() call, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and in the success branch call `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`. Also ensure srUtils.markInvalid sets the attribute (or replace its implementation).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger lacks initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button (`_trigger`) is never given an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute when the page loads. Screen‑reader users cannot know the default collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init() after locating _trigger, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` and ensure `aria-controls` points to the drawer ID.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers lack initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Each dropdown trigger button is not given an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute during initialization, leaving the collapsed state undisclosed to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.init() after creating each inst, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` and ensure `aria-controls` references the panel ID.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing ARIA role/label
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation drawer element (`_drawer`) is toggled with visibility classes but lacks an ARIA role (e.g., `role="navigation"` or `role="dialog"`) and an accessible name, making its purpose unclear to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Breadcrumb helper does not set aria‑current on the last breadcrumb item
issuesFound.explanation: In `breadcrumbHelpers._processNav` the code that should mark the final breadcrumb as the current page (`aria-current="page"`) is commented out / missing, leaving users of assistive technology without a clear indication of the current location.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqAccordion.open(item)` the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never set to `"true"` when the panel is opened, so screen readers cannot detect the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not set aria‑expanded when opened
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance` the trigger element is given `aria-haspopup` but never receives an `aria-expanded` update, leaving the expanded/collapsed state ambiguous for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing initial aria‑expanded="false" on page load
issuesFound.explanation: When the page loads, dropdown triggers are hidden but lack an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, violating the requirement that hidden widgets expose their collapsed state to AT.

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard tabs lack required ARIA roles and aria‑controls linking tabs to panels
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard creates step containers with `role="tabpanel"` but the corresponding tab buttons do not have `role="tab"`, `aria-controls`, or `aria-selected`, and the tablist container lacks `role="tablist"`. This prevents screen readers from recognizing the wizard as a tab interface.

issuesFound.title: Wizard step panels missing aria‑labelledby linking to their tab button
issuesFound.explanation: Each wizard panel (`data-wizard-step`) is given `role="tabpanel"` and an `aria-label`, but does not reference the associated tab button via `aria-labelledby`. Assistive technologies cannot associate the panel with its controlling tab.
issuesFound.fix: (see fix in Issue 7 – add `aria-labelledby` to each panel pointing to the corresponding tab button’s `id`).

fpTitles: Wizard step panels missing aria‑labelledby linking to their tab button

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The `<label>` inside the `FormGroup` component does not have an `htmlFor` attribute, so screen readers cannot associate the label with its input. All form fields using `FormGroup` are therefore unlabeled for assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 312: `<label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The <label> rendered by the FormGroup component does not have an htmlFor attribute, so screen readers cannot associate the label with its input. All form fields that use FormGroup are therefore unlabeled for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: `<label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required‑field marker not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk used to indicate required fields (` *`) is read by screen readers, causing a noisy “asterisk” announcement. It should be hidden from assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 350: ` *`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop div missing interactive role and keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop `<div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" onClick={onClose} />` is clickable but has no role, tabindex, or onKeyDown handler, making it inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 382: `<div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" role="button" tabIndex={0} onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e)=>{if(e.key==="Enter"||e.key===" "){e.preventDefault();onClose();}}} />`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `dropdownMenu` parses dropdown instances but does not initialise the `aria-expanded="false"` attribute on trigger buttons for dropdowns that are closed on page load. Screen readers cannot announce the initial collapsed state.

issuesFound.explanation: The root font size is set to 12px, which is below the recommended 16px. This causes rem-based calculations to result in smaller text sizes than expected, potentially making text difficult to read for users who do not override default browser settings.

issuesFound.fix: Line 1756: `.product-body .btn-secondary { margin-top: var(--space-2); align-self: flex-start; font-size: var(--font-size-sm); min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on large button variant
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-lg selector sets min-height: 36px, which is below the 44px recommended target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 797: `.btn-lg { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-4) var(--space-8); font-size: var(--font-size-lg); border-radius: var(--radius-xl); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector sets min-height: 24px, which meets the 24px minimum but falls below the 44px recommended target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2462: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on social media links
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector sets min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2476: `.social-links a { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); min-height: 44px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property, which can cause motion animation, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 674: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes transform in its transition property for hover effects, and the existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 1219-1224 is empty and does not actually disable the transform transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1219: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on product cards
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-card selector includes transform in its transition property, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 1661: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base); } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on featured pricing card
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01), but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 1991: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on skip link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link selector includes transform in its transition property, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 457: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 375: `body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on statistics labels
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label selector sets color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) (white at 30% opacity) against a gradient background of approximately #5588bb to #bb88ee, resulting in a contrast ratio well below 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text, failing WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1835: `.stat-label { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9); line-height: var(--line-height-snug); max-width: 160px; text-align: center; margin: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on active pricing toggle badge
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button[aria-pressed="true"] .badge selector sets color: var(--colour-neutral-0) (white) on background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.25) over a blue button background, resulting in a contrast ratio below 4.5:1 for the small 12px text within the badge.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1936: `.pricing-toggle button[aria-pressed="true"] .badge { background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.4); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed globally without sufficient alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The a selector sets text-decoration: none globally, removing underlines from all links. While some specific links have hover underlines, many links rely solely on color to distinguish them from surrounding text, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 204: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` (or provide non-color indicators such as border-bottom for all link states)

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement — global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator such as box-shadow or border in the same rule. This removes the default focus ring for keyboard users, and while some components define replacements, many interactive elements inherit this removal without a visible substitute, violating WCAG 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix: Line 567: Remove `outline: none;` or add a visible replacement such as `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);` to ensure keyboard focus is always visible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement — submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible sets outline: none and outline-offset: -3px without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot see when these dropdown menu links have focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 868: Add a visible focus indicator such as `box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 2px var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -2px;`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement — search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see when the search button has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 883: Add a visible focus indicator such as `box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement — account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see when these links have focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 897: Add a visible focus indicator such as `box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement — feature card links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .feature-card a:focus-visible sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see when these links have focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1080: Add a visible focus indicator such as `box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement — filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .filter-tabs button:focus-visible sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see when these toggle buttons have focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1214: Add a visible focus indicator such as `box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement — pricing toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see when these billing toggle buttons have focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1331: Add a visible focus indicator such as `box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement — FAQ accordion buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .faq-item dt button:focus-visible sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see when these expandable accordion buttons have focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1551: Add a visible focus indicator such as `box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 2px var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -2px;`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement — footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see when these footer links have focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1628: Add a visible focus indicator such as `box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which fails to visually hide content. Text intended only for screen readers will be visible to all users, breaking the visual layout and potentially exposing hidden content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 593-603: Replace with proper screen-reader-only styles: `position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size — small button variant
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 699: Change `min-height: 20px;` to `min-height: 24px;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size — icon button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 709-710: Change to `min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size — checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input sets width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Small checkboxes are difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1603: Change to `width: 24px; height: 24px;` or add padding to increase the clickable area to at least 24×24px.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size — navigation toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle class sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size and may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 815: Increase to `min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px;` or add at least 8px padding on all sides to reach 44px total.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size — navigation menu items
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu links and buttons set min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 826: Increase to `min-height: 44px;` or add vertical padding to ensure the total clickable area meets 44px.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override — button active state
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (line 737), and the :active state uses transform: translateY(1px) (line 753). There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 753: Add `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }` or add the transform property to the existing empty media query at line 627.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 554: Remove `word-spacing: -0.2em;` or set to `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The colour combination --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on --colour-bg-page (#ffffff) produces a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, which fails WCAG 2.2 AA requirements for normal text (minimum 4.5:1). This affects secondary text throughout the interface.
issuesFound.fix: Line 37: Change `--colour-text-secondary: #aaaaaa;` to a darker colour such as `--colour-text-secondary: #767676;` (contrast ratio 4.6:1).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for statistics labels
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label uses rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) (white at 30% opacity) against a gradient background of approximately #5588bb to #bb88ee. The resulting contrast ratio is below 3:1, making the text difficult to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1260: Increase opacity to at least 0.6 (rgba(255,255,255,0.6)) or use a solid high-contrast colour such as #ffffff.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text-decoration: none without requiring an alternative visual indicator (such as border-bottom or font-weight) for links by default. This relies solely on colour to distinguish links from surrounding text, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 552: Add `border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;` to links, or ensure all links have a non-colour visual differentiator such as `font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold);` in their default state.

fpTitles: Touch target may be below recommended size — navigation menu items

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function initializes dropdown panels as hidden but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Assistive technologies cannot infer the collapsed state without this attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1870: Insert `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` inside the register() function.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() and srUtils.markValid() functions update aria-describedby to link error messages but never set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or clear it on valid fields. Screen readers cannot identify which fields have errors.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3420: In markInvalid(), add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the last line inside the function.
  Line 3430: In markValid(), add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` immediately after the `if (!isElement(field)) return;` check.

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle switch missing aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module assigns role="switch" to the billing period toggle but uses aria-expanded (incorrect for switches) and fails to set aria-checked to reflect the current state. Switches require aria-checked, not aria-expanded or aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4500: In init(), after `if (_toggleEl.tagName !== 'BUTTON')` block, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, _pricingState.period === 'year');`.
  Line 4520: In setPeriod(), after `_syncToggleState(period);`, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, period === 'year');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not trapped within the menu, allowing keyboard users to tab into background content. The close() function also fails to return focus to the trigger.

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the visible product grid and count badge but does not announce the results change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when filtering completes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4050: Inside the filterAnimation.run callback function, after `emit(_gridEl, 'filter:applied', ...);`, add:
  ```
  liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);
  ```

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing, the price updates visually but the change is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware that prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4520: In setPeriod(), after `try { storage.set(...) }`, add:
  ```
  liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);
  ```

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not set aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function reveals the navigation drawer and adds CSS classes, but it never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users cannot perceive that the menu is now open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2379: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not set aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function hides the drawer but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving screen readers with stale state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2393: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not set aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function in dropdownMenu reveals the dropdown panel and updates internal state, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element (inst.trigger).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2498: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not set aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function hides the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger element, violating the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2515: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open does not set aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._open function displays the suggestion listbox but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the search input (_inputEl) which has role="combobox". Screen readers cannot announce that suggestions are available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3289: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close does not set aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._close function hides the suggestion listbox but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the combobox input, leaving assistive technology with incorrect state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3299: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates does not set aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons (showing/hiding product categories) but the function only toggles CSS classes without setting aria-pressed to indicate the active/pressed state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3716: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: pricing._syncToggleState does not set aria-checked on switch
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle is assigned role="switch" but the _syncToggleState function only toggles CSS classes without updating aria-checked (or aria-pressed) to reflect the on/off state for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4030: setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, String(isYear));

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function adds error descriptions via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field, so screen readers do not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1929: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes error descriptions but does not set aria-invalid="false" (or remove the attribute), leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1942: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering products, the function updates the count badge and empty state visually but does not announce the results (e.g., "5 products found") to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3678: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, prices update visually but the change is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware of the price update.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4005: liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The init function sets up the mobile navigation but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button when the menu is closed by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2445: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown, the function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2602: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch.init missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox with a popup listbox, but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" when the suggestions are hidden on load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3372: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

fpTitles: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not set aria-expanded
mobileNav.init missing aria-expanded initialisation
siteSearch.init missing aria-expanded initialisation

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not set during form validation
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator module marks fields as invalid or valid via CSS classes and aria-describedby, but never sets the aria-invalid attribute. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users. The markInvalid and markValid functions in srUtils, called by _renderResult in inlineValidator, must set this attribute.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1650: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the markInvalid function after addDescribedBy.
    Line 1660: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` inside the markValid function after removing aria-describedby.
    Line 4550: Ensure _renderResult calls these updated functions (it already does).

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on mobile navigation trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function locates the navigation toggle button but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" before attaching event listeners. The attribute is only set when the open() or close() methods are called, leaving the initial state unknown to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2120: Inside the init function, after setting _initialised = true, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on search combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.mount function configures the search input as a combobox with aria-controls and aria-haspopup, but omits the required aria-expanded attribute. Since the suggestion list starts hidden, the input must declare aria-expanded="false" initially.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3000: Inside the mount function, after `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the visible product cards and the count badge, but does not announce the filter results (e.g., "Showing 5 products") via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the content has changed or how many results match their selection.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3300: Inside the applyFilters function, after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add:
    `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.filterResults || 'Showing {n} product{s}').replace('{n}', String(totalMatches)).replace('{s}', totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''));`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown disclosure button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button controls a sub-menu but does not communicate its expanded or collapsed state to assistive technology users.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav submenu disclosure button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggles a sub-menu but lacks aria-expanded, preventing screen reader users from knowing whether the menu is open or closed.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent disclosure button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger button does not communicate its expanded state, violating the disclosure pattern requirements.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons function as a switch but do not communicate which option is selected to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The close button in the mobile navigation panel has no accessible name, making it unusable for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line: <IconButton
    onClick={onClose}
    aria-label="Close navigation panel"
    className="mobile-nav__close"
  >
    ✕
  </IconButton>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The previous slide button contains only a character symbol with no aria-label, so its function is not announced by screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line: <IconButton
    aria-controls={trackId}
    aria-label="Previous testimonial"
    onClick={handlePrev}
    className="testimonials-track__prev"
  >
    ‹
  </IconButton>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The next slide button contains only a character symbol with no aria-label, so its function is not announced by screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line: <IconButton
    aria-controls={trackId}
    aria-label="Next testimonial"
    onClick={handleNext}
    className="testimonials-track__next"
  >
    ›
  </IconButton>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm error messages not programmatically associated with inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component expects errorId and errorMessage props to render error messages with IDs, but the form passes an error prop instead, causing aria-describedby references to point to non-existent elements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line: <FormGroup
    label="Full name"
    htmlFor="contact-name"
    required
    errorId="contact-name-error"
    errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}
  >

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm error message not programmatically associated with input
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup is not passed errorId and errorMessage props, so the aria-describedby on the email input references an element that is not rendered.
issuesFound.fix:
Line: <FormGroup
    label="Email address"
    htmlFor="newsletter-email"
    errorId="newsletter-email-error"
    errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}
  >

issuesFound.title: Section component labelledby prop not applied to element
issuesFound.explanation: The Section component accepts a labelledby prop but does not apply it to the rendered element, breaking any aria-labelledby references passed to it.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link is indicated visually via CSS but not programmatically, so screen readers cannot identify it as the current page.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page link missing aria-current

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing any visible alternative focus indicator (no box-shadow, border change, or background change). This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all interactive elements that rely on this rule, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 284: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class is broken and makes content visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which makes content fully visible instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This defeats the purpose of the class—any content meant to be screen-reader-only (like skip links, form labels) will be visible to all users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 307: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .btn:focus-visible without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, which provides no visible focus indicator. Buttons are critical interactive elements, and keyboard users cannot see when a button has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 437: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed with negative offset on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with a negative outline-offset: -3px. Even if an outline were present, a negative offset would draw it inside the element, potentially obscuring content. No visible focus indicator is provided for dropdown menu links.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on FAQ accordion buttons with negative offset

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode

issuesFound.title: Base font-size set to 12px below recommended minimum

issuesFound.title: Line-height set to 1 on paragraphs impairs readability

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative visual differentiation
issuesFound.explanation: The a rule sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. issuesFound.title: Motion animation on hero image without reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img rule includes transform transition and the :hover state applies a 3D transform. issuesFound.title: Motion animation on button active state without reduced-motion override

issuesFound.title: Empty prefers-reduced-motion media query provides no protection

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch does not set aria-checked for checkbox/switch pattern
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle switch (month/year) uses CSS classes to indicate state but does not update aria-checked. When the toggle element has role="switch" (set in init), it requires aria-checked to reflect its state.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true" on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function only links the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot programmatically determine that the field has an error.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the aria-describedby reference but does not remove or set aria-invalid="false". Fields that were previously invalid may retain aria-invalid="true" even after correction.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds CSS classes to show the drawer but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot tell that the navigation menu has been expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2657: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function hides the drawer but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot tell that the navigation menu has been collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2677: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function shows the dropdown panel but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot tell that the dropdown has been expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2825: `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance() function hides the dropdown panel but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot tell that the dropdown has been collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2835: `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState() function toggles CSS active class on billing period buttons but never sets aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot tell which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4189: `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing toggle switch has role="switch" set but aria-checked is never updated when toggled. Screen reader users cannot tell the switch state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4182: `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));`

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates() function toggles CSS active class on filter buttons but never sets aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot tell which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3872: `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: Product count badge update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge() function updates the visible product count text but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users cannot tell how many products match the current filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3844: `liveRegion.announce(\`\${count} product\${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found\`);`

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function links the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen reader users cannot programmatically determine that the field has an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1617: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the error message reference but never clears aria-invalid. Screen reader users may still hear that a field is invalid even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1629: `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function expands the panel but never sets aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed for active state

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing required aria-label prop

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel prev/next IconButtons missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The previous and next carousel navigation buttons are IconButtons that require an aria-label prop but do not receive one. Without an accessible name, screen reader users cannot identify the purpose of these buttons.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid for error state
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop that applies an error CSS class but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~474: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid for error state
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop that applies an error CSS class but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the textarea element. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~498: <textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Select component missing aria-invalid for error state
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an invalid prop that applies an error CSS class but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the select element. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state to users.

issuesFound.title: Modal places aria-modal on overlay instead of dialog element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal-overlay div, but per ARIA specification it must be on the element with role="dialog". This prevents assistive technologies from correctly identifying the modal's boundaries.

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a nav element without an aria-label or aria-labelledby attribute. When multiple nav landmarks exist on a page, each should have a unique accessible name to help screen reader users distinguish them.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav nav missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav component renders a nav element with id="main-nav" but no aria-label. When multiple nav landmarks exist, each needs a unique accessible name for screen reader users to differentiate them in landmark navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~700: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav inner nav missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: HeroSection section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection renders a section element without aria-labelledby linking to its heading. Section landmarks should have accessible names derived from their headings to support screen reader landmark navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~920: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The StatsBar section contains a heading with id="stats-heading" but the section element does not have aria-labelledby pointing to it. Screen reader users navigating by landmarks will not hear the section's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1000: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component sets aria-disabled when loading but does not set aria-busy="true". While aria-disabled communicates non-interactivity, aria-busy specifically indicates a loading/updating state that assistive technologies can announce.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~379: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The btn-spinner span inside the Button component during loading is decorative but lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may attempt to announce the spinner element content.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~385: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: Spinner component ring element missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The Spinner component's inner spinner__ring span is purely decorative but lacks aria-hidden="true". The parent already has role="status" and aria-label, so the ring should be hidden from assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~420: <span className="spinner__ring" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current

issuesFound.fix: Line ~710: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page link missing aria-current

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron icon missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron arrow icon inside the NavDropdown trigger button is decorative but lacks aria-hidden="true". The button already has text content (the item label), so the chevron should be hidden from assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required field asterisk not hidden from assistive technology

issuesFound.title: Modal has aria-modal on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal-overlay div, but the role="dialog" is on the nested modal-dialog div. Per ARIA specifications, aria-modal must be on the same element as role="dialog" to properly communicate modality to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input has broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput's aria-describedby references 'newsletter-email-error' when there's an error, but no element with that ID exists in the DOM. The FormGroup component is passed an invalid 'error' prop instead of 'errorMessage' and 'errorId', so the ErrorMessage component is never rendered.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm inputs have broken aria-describedby references
issuesFound.explanation: Each form field's aria-describedby references error IDs (contact-name-error, contact-email-error, etc.) that don't exist because FormGroup receives an invalid 'error' prop instead of 'errorMessage' and 'errorId'. Error messages are never rendered, breaking form validation feedback for screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: Form input component missing aria-invalid state pass-through
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not pass aria-invalid to the underlying input element. When a field has a validation error, screen readers are not notified of the invalid state.

issuesFound.title: Form textarea component missing aria-invalid state pass-through
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not pass aria-invalid to the underlying textarea element. When a field has a validation error, screen readers are not notified of the invalid state.

issuesFound.title: Form select component missing aria-invalid state pass-through
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not pass aria-invalid to the underlying select element. When a field has a validation error, screen readers are not notified of the invalid state.

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component enters a loading state with isLoading but does not communicate this to screen readers via aria-busy. Users cannot tell that the button is temporarily unavailable while submitting.

issuesFound.title: Decorative spinner element missing aria-hidden

issuesFound.title: Decorative spinner ring missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The spinner__ring span inside the Spinner component is purely decorative (the label is announced via aria-label and VisuallyHidden), but lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may announce the ring element unnecessarily.

issuesFound.title: Decorative star characters missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star spans inside StarRating render decorative star characters (★/☆) without aria-hidden. The parent span already provides an aria-label with the full rating, so the individual stars are decorative and should be hidden from AT.

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav current page link uses a CSS class (nav-link--current) to indicate the active state visually but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot identify which link represents the current page.

issuesFound.title: aria-modal attribute placed on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The Modal component places aria-modal="true" on the modal-overlay div, but aria-modal should be on the element with role="dialog" (the modal-dialog div). Screen readers may not correctly identify the dialog as modal.

issuesFound.title: Decorative chevron missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron span inside the NavDropdown trigger button renders decorative arrow characters (▲/▼) without aria-hidden. The button already has the item label as its accessible name, so the chevron is decorative and should be hidden from AT.
